title: Rotate long-lived credentials on a schedule
description: Give credentials a finite lifetime and rotate them on a defined cadence so a quiet compromise cannot persist indefinitely.
body: Use when a credential has no expiry or has been valid for a long time. Do not let a key live forever because nothing has broken. Actions: define a rotation cadence per credential class; prefer short-lived dynamically issued credentials where the manager supports them; automate rotation so it is not a manual chore. Validation signals include credentials carrying expiry and the manager showing recent rotation. Risk boundary: a never-rotated key turns any past exposure into a permanent breach, so do not advise extending lifetimes for convenience and escalate exceptions to a human.
